layout.grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 gap-6

NewsCard.style: bg-card border border-border rounded-sm hover:border-primary/50 transition-colors duration-300 flex flex-col overflow-hidden group
NewsCard.image_style: w-full h-48 object-cover grayscale group-hover:grayscale-0 transition-all duration-500
NewsCard.badge_style: inline-flex items-center rounded-sm border px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2 border-transparent bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80

CountryFilter.style: flex flex-wrap gap-2 p-4 border-b border-border sticky top-0 bg-background/95 backdrop-blur z-50

Header.style: border-b border-border py-6 mb-8 flex justify-between items-center

general: Use 'JetBrains Mono' for metadata (dates, source names).
Implement a 'Bento Grid' layout where high-priority stories (Politics/Disaster) span 2 columns.
Ensure Dark Mode is default and permanent (force class='dark' on html/body).
Use the provided noise overlay for a 'print' texture feel.
Countries to filter: UK, Ireland, Portugal, Iceland, Moldova, Latvia, Lithuania, Estonia, Finland, Norway, Sweden, Cape Verde.
Auto-refresh functionality must be visible (e.g., a progress bar or countdown).
Do not use gradients for backgrounds. Use solid Zinc-950 (#09090b).
Add data-testid attributes to all interactive elements.

components.StoryCard: Must display: Title, Summary (truncated), Source Badge, Country Tag, Relevance Score, Time ago.
components.SourceDiversity: Visual indicator (e.g., progress bar or pie chart) showing distribution of sources.
components.PriorityTags: Politics (Orange), Disaster (Blue). High relevance scores should visually highlight the card.

universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic outputs. Avoid this.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
Subtle grain textures separate good from extraordinary.
